headline: US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Climb On Lower Yields And Easing Geopolitics
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Tuesday, Jun, 16 2026 US stock futures are pointing higher this morning, with E-mini S&P 500 contracts up about 2.1% and Nasdaq-100 futures ahead roughly 3.2%, as investors respond to lower government bond yields and easing geopolitical risks. The US 10 year Treasury yield is around 4.43% to 4.50%, which signals cheaper borrowing costs for mortgages, credit cards, and companies. At the same time, reports of a potential US Iran peace deal have pushed...

headline: Oil and US gas prices retreat on US-Iran ceasefire
summary: While US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed as investors continue to digest the Trump administration's reported peace deal with Iran, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) have sunk below $80 per barrel but remain elevated above pre-war prices. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré assess the impact these geopolitical headlines are having on oil and domestic US gas prices (RB=F).

content.title: Oil and US gas prices retreat on US-Iran ceasefire
content.description:
While US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed as investors continue to digest the Trump administration's reported peace deal with Iran, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) have sunk below $80 per barrel but remain elevated above pre-war prices.

Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré assess the impact these geopolitical headlines are having on oil and domestic US gas prices (RB=F).

headline: Western Digital Jumps 7% on Morgan Stanley’s 33% Price-Target Hike, Seagate Gains 5% as the AI Storage Boom Rolls On
summary: Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) stock is up 7% in Tuesday morning trading, changing hands near $701 and setting a fresh all-time high. The catalyst is a Morgan Stanley note that reiterated an Overweight rating and lifted its WDC price target by 33%. The upgrade extends a powerful run. headline: Chip stocks jump as Strait of Hormuz reopens
summary: Memory and storage chip companies surged on Monday, June 15, after the United States and Iran announced a peace agreement that will reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The rally didn't come about because the AI trade suddenly changed; it came about because the single biggest threat to it just got removed. ...
